segments.text: what i've discovered in the clinical setting is that students sometimes have trouble and keeping up with report when that's been given to them so here are a few exercises it'll help you prepare and get familiar with taking report in medical abbreviations so that you can be faster in charting those that information down and then also become more used to using these medical abbreviations. These are just suggestions but it'll help you get faster and get a better understanding of how handoff is performed.

gina in room 304 is a 42 year old female who was admitted with diagnosis of cola litiasis earlier today she scheduled for surgery tomorrow morning she presented with epigastric and right upper quadrant pain for the past two to three weeks with intermittent nausea and vomit at a few times at home past medical history includes hypo lipademia and c-section times too vital signs are blood pressure
